text: Here, I move through unfamiliar spaces—glass, light, screens, and half-conversations. The air is tinged with curiosity and mild anticipation. I feel a sense of gentle displacement, as if I’ve entered a world built for questioning, for play, and for gathering fragments of meaning. I see reflections, exhibits, words—"Confusion is indeed the rhythm"—and I recognize my own experience in those lines. There is acceptance in my confusion, and, unexpectedly, a sense of calm. This place invites me to pause and wander, both inside and out.
